factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to industrial capital expenditure deferrals.

evidence.text: During 2016 and the first six months of 2017, our core sales have been challenged by broad-based industrial capital expenditure and maintenance deferrals.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to industrial capital expenditure deferrals, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Order backlog is shrinking due to declines in project orders.

evidence.text: We continue to experience declines in project orders, particularly within the energy and infrastructure businesses.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declines in project orders, particularly within energy and infrastructure businesses, suggest a negative trend.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable with selective price increases to mitigate inflation.

evidence.text: We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is stable with selective increases to mitigate inflation, showing no clear directional signal.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to core revenue decline.

evidence.text: Although we saw early signs of recovery in the first six months of 2017, we expect this challenge to continue throughout 2017.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to core revenue decline, indicating a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining, particularly in project sales.

evidence.text: Lower project core sales volumes in the Electrical segment, which resulted in decreased leverage on fixed expenses included in cost of goods sold.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining, particularly in project sales, which is a clear negative signal.

factors.summary: End markets are mixed, with energy and infrastructure slowing while residential and commercial are growing.

evidence.text: Core sales growth in our residential & commercial business, primarily as a result of increased volumes in the U.S.
evidence.subsection: Net sales

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from end markets, with some sectors growing while others slow, resulting in a neutral assessment.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, with the company attempting to offset through pricing and productivity.

evidence.text: We have experienced material and other cost inflation. We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs are being partially offset by pricing and productivity improvements, leading to a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Labor costs are creating margin pressure due to wage inflation.

evidence.text: Inflationary increases related to labor costs and certain raw materials; and lower core sales volumes in our energy and infrastructure businesses, which resulted in decreased leverage on operating expenses.
evidence.subsection: Segment income

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are creating margin pressure due to wage inflation, indicating a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Margins are improving due to productivity and cost control measures.

evidence.text: The 1.2 and 0.3 percentage point increase s in gross profit as a percentage of sales in the second quarter and first half, respectively, of 2017 from 2016 were primarily driven by: higher contribution margin as a result of savings generated from our Pentair Integrated Management System (PIMS) initiatives including lean and supply management practices; and selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear evidence of significant improvement in margins due to productivity and cost control.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: We continued execution of certain business restructuring initiatives aimed at reducing our fixed cost structure and realigning our business to offset the negative earnings impact of core revenue decline and foreign exchange. We expect these actions will contribute to margin growth in 2017.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Measurable savings from cost reduction programs contributing to margin growth.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through lean initiatives and supply management.

evidence.text: The 1.2 and 0.3 percentage point increase s in gross profit as a percentage of sales in the second quarter and first half, respectively, of 2017 from 2016 were primarily driven by: higher contribution margin as a result of savings generated from our Pentair Integrated Management System (PIMS) initiatives including lean and supply management practices; and selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in productivity through lean initiatives and supply management.

factors.summary: Management is cautious with actions like debt reduction and operational excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: Our operating objectives include reducing long-term debt and overall leverage through improved cash flow performance; driving operating excellence through lean enterprise initiatives, with specific focus on sourcing and supply management, cash flow management and lean operations.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cautious approach with specific objectives, but no clear positive or negative sentiment expressed.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include foreign currency effects and economic conditions impacting demand.

evidence.text: We expect this trend to continue throughout 2017. The current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign currency effects and economic conditions pose moderate risks.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on innovation and market expansion.

evidence.text: Our operating objectives include achieving differentiated revenue growth through new products and global and market expansion; and optimizing our technological capabilities to increasingly generate innovative new products.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on innovation and market expansion indicates significant positive developments.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange rates and economic conditions.

evidence.text: Unfavorable foreign currency effects for the three and six months ended June 30, 2017. The current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange rates and economic conditions present clear risks.

factors.summary: Catalysts include the proposed separation and new product launches.

evidence.text: The Proposed Separation is expected to occur through a tax-free spin-off of the Electrical business. We are reinforcing our businesses to more effectively address these opportunities through research and development and additional sales and marketing resources.
evidence.subsection: Overview

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Proposed separation and new product launches indicate major positive developments.

factors.summary: CapEx is being maintained, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Reducing long-term debt and overall leverage through improved cash flow performance; driving operating excellence through lean enterprise initiatives, with specific focus on sourcing and supply management, cash flow management and lean operations.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Maintaining CapEx signals confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving due to debt repayments.

evidence.text: The 28.5 and 15.8 percent decrease s in net interest expense in the second quarter and first half, respectively, of 2017 from 2016 were primarily driven by: the impact of lower debt levels during the second quarter and first half of 2017, compared to the second quarter and first half of 2016. In May 2017, the proceeds from the sale of the Valves & Controls business were utilized to repay all commercial paper and revolving long term debt and for the early extinguishment of $1,659.3 million of certain series of fixed rate debt.
evidence.subsection: Net interest expense

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant debt repayments indicate strong financial health.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving due to cost reductions and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The 1.2 and 0.3 percentage point increase s in gross profit as a percentage of sales in the second quarter and first half, respectively, of 2017 from 2016 were primarily driven by: higher contribution margin as a result of savings generated from our Pentair Integrated Management System (PIMS) initiatives including lean and supply management practices; and selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases.
evidence.subsection: Gross profit

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement due to cost reductions and efficiency.

factors.summary: MA activity is focused on divestitures and acquisitions to drive growth.

evidence.text: The sale of our Valves & Controls business to Emerson Electric Co. for $3.15 billion. The results of the Valves & Controls business have been presented as discontinued operations and the related assets and liabilities have been reclassified as held for sale for all periods presented.
evidence.subsection: Overview

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strategic divestitures and acquisitions for growth.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind with industrial slowdowns impacting demand.

evidence.text: Despite the favorable long-term outlook for our end-markets, we experience differing levels of volatility depending on the end-market and may continue to do so over the medium and longer term.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Industrial slowdowns impact demand negatively.

factors.summary: Interest rate increases are a headwind due to higher debt service costs.

evidence.text: The 28.5 and 15.8 percent decrease s in net interest expense in the second quarter and first half, respectively, of 2017 from 2016 were primarily driven by: the impact of lower debt levels during the second quarter and first half of 2017, compared to the second quarter and first half of 2016. In May 2017, the proceeds from the sale of the Valves & Controls business were utilized to repay all commercial paper and revolving long term debt and for the early extinguishment of $1,659.3 million of certain series of fixed rate debt.
evidence.subsection: Net interest expense

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher debt service costs due to rate increases.

factors.summary: FX exposure is a risk with unfavorable effects impacting results.

evidence.text: Unfavorable foreign currency effects for the three and six months ended June 30, 2017. The current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable foreign currency effects impacting results.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is occurring through layoffs and back-office consolidation.

evidence.text: The 0.1 percentage point decrease in SG&A expense as a percentage of sales in the second quarter of 2017 from 2016 was primarily driven by: cost control and savings generated from back-office consolidation, reduction in personnel and other lean initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / Forward-looking Statements

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Layoffs and back-office consolidation indicate cost-cutting.
